definitions: A networking connection between two Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs) that allows them to communicate as if they are within the same network.
A method to connect two VPCs, even if they belong to different projects or organizations, enabling private IP communication between resources in both networks.
examples: We configured VPC Network Peering to allow our development and production environments to communicate.
VPC Network Peering simplifies data transfer between microservices in separate VPCs.
Security groups apply to traffic traversing VPC Network Peering connections.
